title: Exceptions Introduction
description: What are exceptions and why use them?

slides.title: Exceptions: A Better Way
slides.content:
# What are Exceptions?

Exceptions are an object-oriented way to handle errors. They allow you to separate error handling code from regular logic. When an exceptional situation occurs, you **throw** an exception. You then **catch** it elsewhere.

Benefits:
*   Cleaner code
*   Can propagate errors up the call stack
*   Can have different catch blocks for different exception types

In PHP, exceptions are objects of class `Exception` (or its subclasses).
